# addypin

> Turn GPS coordinates into a short, memorable link or email address —
> `SHORTCODE.addypin.com` (web) and `SHORTCODE@addypin.com` (email). No
> accounts, no tracking, open source. Coordinates are encrypted at rest and
> owner identities are HMAC-derived, so the server can't read your pins
> without its keys.

## Pages
- [addypin](https://addypin.com/): drop a pin on a map, get a short link or email address.

## How it works
- Drop a pin on the map → get `SHORTCODE.addypin.com` to share and `SHORTCODE@addypin.com` to look up by email.
- A public lookup returns only `{ lat, lng, map links }` — no owner, no timestamps, no labels.
- All management is via magic link — no passwords, no OAuth.
- Unconfirmed pins auto-delete after 72 hours; a shortcode is never reused once deleted or expired.

## Privacy invariant
- Only encrypted latitude/longitude is stored per pin. No labels, no analytics, no session table.
- Pin pages are `noindex` and the coordinates API is disallowed to crawlers, so no location data is ever indexed.
